name: ColBERT (Contextualized Late Interaction)
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); ColBERT from Stanford; RAGatouille wrapper by bclavie

auth.notes: Local inference; HuggingFace Hub for model download

performance.notes: Late interaction: precompute document embeddings; query time: fast MaxSim operation; GPU needed for index building

agent_readiness.notes: Late interaction retrieval model achieving near cross-encoder accuracy with bi-encoder speed. RAGatouille is the easy-to-use wrapper. ColBERT encodes query and documents to token-level embeddings; MaxSim interaction at query time. Significantly better retrieval than cosine similarity bi-encoders. RAGatouille: RAGPretrainedModel wraps ColBERT-v2; index(), search() API. Integrated with LangChain and LlamaIndex. Best accuracy/speed tradeoff for RAG retrieval.

gotchas: Index building requires GPU (CPU is impractically slow for large collections)
Larger index footprint than bi-encoder (token-level embeddings)
RAGatouille is v0.0.x — API still evolving
Less community support than mainstream retrieval approaches
best_when: RAG pipelines needing better retrieval accuracy than cosine similarity
When cross-encoder reranking latency is too high
Long documents where token-level interaction matters
avoid_when: Simple retrieval tasks where BM25 + embedding is sufficient
summary: ColBERT (via RAGatouille) provides late interaction retrieval — token-level embeddings with MaxSim at query time, achieving near cross-encoder accuracy at bi-encoder speeds. RAGatouille wraps ColBERT-v2 with index() and search() APIs and integrates with LangChain/LlamaIndex. Best accuracy/speed tradeoff for RAG retrieval. Requires GPU for index building. LangChain/LlamaIndex integration available.
